calendar.matters:
ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-21-2021 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Bassi, via ZOOM, for Daniela Jonguitud, via ZOOM, Successor Trustee.

Mr. Bassi provides advocacy for why neither he nor his client appeared at the previous calling of this matter which prompted the court to issue an order to appear for today. The court is satisfied with Mr. Bassi's report and the order to appear is taken off calendar. The court then turns to the number of Examiner's notes which require attention. Mr. Bassi reports on the total number of hours spent predating the decedent's death, including speaking with the decedent's attorney, and organizing Ms. Jonguitud's trustee expenses. Mr. Bassi provides additional testimony in support of the request for trustee fees and his fees.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: D. Xiao. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Successor Trustee Daniela Jonguitud and the Attorney Micheal Bassi to appear on November 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear. The Petition for Settlement of Second and Final Account and Report of Trsutee, etc. is also continued to that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Lee. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-25-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-19-302587 Conservatorship of Eda Shostac and
PTR-19-302694 The Shostac Living Trust.

Appearances: Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for and with Minna Fernan (Conservator) by zoom.
Donna Quan appeared by zoom for Daniela Jonguitud (Trustee).

The Court asked Ms. Quan if she agrees that the Trustee's bond should be reduced to $651,000.00, rather than $510,000.00, and how much time they need to submit the final accountings. Ms. Quan stated that she is alright with it. Mr. Schultz stated that he doesn't think it will take a lot of time to do the accountings. Ms. Quan stated that she thinks it will take about 45 to 60-days. The Court stated that it has concerns about Mr. Bassi's fee request, that there are a lot of things here that should've been done by the Fiduciary, not by Counsel and not at Counsel rate. Mr. Schultz stated that he has no specific concerns. Ms. Quan stated that the accounting was done by a third party, that they had to expend Attorney time because the bank refused to follow the Court order, that it took time, and that they had to prepare declarations and answer questions raised by the Probate Examiner. Ms. Quan also stated that they had difficulty with Frist Republic Bank, that First Republic was not cooperating, that they worked with a financial institution to get the Trustee bonded and that there was a credit history issue. The Court stated that it should have been made aware of the bond issue and the hours spent talking to the banks. Ms. Quan stated that she leaves it at the discretion of Court as to the fees, that she has nothing to add regarding special notice to Anthony Sciortino, that Mr. Sciortino is neither a Beneficiary of the trust, nor a remainder Beneficiary of the estate, and that he has no right. The Court stated that it hasn't decided on the issue of notice, but it is thinking about the family relationship. Ms. Quan stated that she would also like to petition for termination of Court supervision of the trust. Mr. Schultz stated that when his Client came in she monitored the Conservatee's care, that his Client feels that the care agency's rates commensurate with the Bay Area rates and that the care services that were provided were superior. Mr. Schultz also stated that they haven't had any communication with the family, that the family never visited the Conservatee and that the family really refused to make any arrangements for the disposition of the Conservatee's remains. The First Account and Report of Conservator, etc. and the First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. are under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Confirming Appointment Of Daniela Jonguitud As Successor Trustee Of The Shostac Living Trust
Off Calendar May-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Bassi for Daniela Jonguitud (Petitioner).
Andrew Schultz for Minna Fernan (Temporary Conservator of the Settlor).
Bruce Feder Court appointed Counsel for Beneficiary.

Mr. Feder and Mr. Schultz stated that they are not here to object to the pending petition. Mr. Bassi stated that he is not aware of any objections. Mr. Schultz stated that his Client marshalled the bank account receiving the social security benefits with a balance of $20,000.00. Mr. Bassi estimates that there is about one million dollars in the trust. Discussion was held regarding trust provisions, Ms. Shostac's medical condition, Mr. Shostac having authority acting as sole trustee to amend the trust, the issue of the Successor Trustee being bonded, concern about the dual roll of trustee and employer of the caregivers, the issue of Mr. Feder's appointment being expanded to participate in the trust aspect, the request for special notice made by a non-beneficiary, etc. Mr. Feder stated that he prefers that the Successor Trustee be bonded when she has access to the funds. Mr. Feder can file something to expand his appointment if he wants to. The Court granted the Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
